answer:
To authenticate with the Twilio SendGrid v3 API, you must use an API key provided in an HTTP Authorization header as a Bearer token [1][2][3].

Include the header in your request as follows:
Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_API_KEY_HERE>

For example, using cURL:
```bash
curl -X "GET" "https://api.sendgrid.com/v3/templates" \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_API_KEY_HERE>" \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json"
```

Twilio SendGrid no longer accepts account username and password credentials for API authentication [4][5]. For SMTP integrations, use the username apikey and your generated API key as the password [2][5]. You must also enable two-factor authentication on your account [2][6].
